request: Design a single-page promo site for revüe itself, from the repo's own brand source, through the full v1.0 pipeline.

inputs.note: the design source: locked palette (6 colors), typography rules, logo usage
wordmark reference for the hero treatment

brief.deliverable: Single-file HTML promo one-pager for revüe, desktop + mobile breakpoints

hardNos.why: brand voice: never generic link text
the name is revüe (u-umlaut) — never flatten it in display copy

options.summary: Verdict-led: the four verdict states are the visual spine; navy hero, coral ü, verdict cards in their brand colors.
Gate-pipeline: a horizontal strip of the operating contract's gates, developer-tool aesthetic, monochrome navy with aqua accents.
Before/after split: messy work on the left, clean handoff on the right, transformation narrative.

optionFeedback.note: verdict cards are the most revüe-specific asset; keep as the spine
keep the gate strip, fold it into concept A below the verdicts instead of leading with it
before/after split is a generic marketing trope — fails the anti-generic bar

evidence.statement: Read docs/brand.md, assets/logo-revue.svg, and README.md this session before writing the brief; the lock's six hex values are copied from docs/brand.md verbatim.
Ran scripts/validate-run.py on the incomplete-brief run first (examples/dogfood/run-incomplete-brief.json) — the brief gate blocked with one batched field request, exit code 1, observed this session.
Ran scripts/validate-output.py on winner-draft-1.html this session: REJECTED, exit code 1 — caught #e64435, a guessed darker-coral hover, labeled near-miss of #e5484d.
Ran scripts/validate-output.py on winner.html this session: 6 colors found, all in lock; fonts in lock; no Hard-NO hits; no unverified claims; exit code 0. Audit object attached below verbatim.
Spacing/grid and logo-usage lock fields are not machine-checked in v1.0; verified by eye only. Page renders unchecked in real browsers (no screenshot in this environment).

scorecard.evidence: 640px breakpoint present; flex layouts collapse to column
Static review only in this session — no rendered-browser screenshot
Output audit: all 6 colors and both fonts in lock
File path shared; opens in any browser
Single file, sectioned CSS, comments name the concept lineage

verdict.reason: Brief was complete before generation, three distinct lock-compliant concepts were presented, the winner was rebuilt from keep/kill/change feedback, and the output audit passed clean after catching a real color drift in draft 1. One bounded change remains: render in a real browser and confirm the preview before publishing.
verdict.nextAction: Open examples/dogfood/winner.html in a browser, confirm both breakpoints, then publish.

pathToShip.item: Open winner.html in a real browser and confirm desktop + mobile rendering matches intent
